repository.description: Sim Racing Venue Management System

commit.message:
Fix blocking issues in course modules 3, 5, 7, 8

Module 3: fix poisson_deviance zero-handling (np.where instead of
1e-10 inside log ratio); correct feature importance label from
'permutation' to 'PredictionValuesChange' and description from
'mean absolute SHAP value' to the correct metric name.

Module 5: add explicit caveat to portfolio reserve range that the
symmetric normal approximation understates variance for asymmetric
Tweedie intervals; fix minimum premium floor explanation - the 90th
percentile upper bound is exceeded 10% of the time, not a guarantee
of coverage in bad years.

Module 7: fix cross-subsidy analysis - uniform factor multipliers
produce identical percentage changes for all policies; reframe around
absolute premium changes by segment and make the limitation explicit.

Module 8: replace weight=exposure with baseline=np.log(exposure) for
all CatBoost Poisson frequency model Pool constructions (CV, Optuna
tuning, full training); define best_sev_params before use in Stage 6;
replace random 80/20 conformal calibration split with temporal split
on most recent accident year, consistent with Module 5 teaching.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Phase 7 — US3 Optional Relay Mode (T044-T046):
- RouterCommunicationService gRPC: relay forwarding between peers
- Conditional mapping via --enable-relay flag

Phase 8 — Polish (T047-T056):
- Dockerfile (3-stage: build, permissions, chiseled runtime)
- launchSettings.json with PeerRouter and PeerRouter-Relay profiles
- RouterConfiguration: environment variable support (PEERROUTER__*), NodeName
- Extensive README.md with full configuration, architecture, and deployment guide
- Documentation updated: MASTER-TASKS, PORT-CONFIGURATION, development-status
- Peer Service README updated with circuit breaker and PostgreSQL queue changes

PeerRouter is standalone — NOT part of docker-compose or Aspire AppHost.
77 PeerRouter tests passing. Feature 053 fully complete (56/56 tasks).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Refactor JDBC tests to use shared test factories with multi-database support (#27)

* Refactor JDBC tests to use shared test factories with multi-database support

Restructure JDBC tests to mirror the R2DBC module's test architecture:
- Add JdbcTestDialect interface with 7 dialect implementations (H2, Postgres, MySQL, MariaDB, MSSQL, Oracle)
- Extract 6 reusable test factories (basicInsertQuery, transaction, dataType, namedParam, parameterMapper, queryingForPrimitives)
- Add Testcontainer-based helpers for Postgres, MySQL, MariaDB, MSSQL, and Oracle
- Create per-database test classes that compose all factories

Also improves JDBC production code:
- ResultSetReader: add DOUBLE/FLOAT/REAL, NUMERIC, CHAR/NCHAR variants, BINARY, Types.OTHER support; fix BIGINT to use getLong; add wasNull() null handling
- JdbcTransaction: add LocalDate and UUID parameter binding
- DefaultConversions: add java.sql.Date→LocalDate, ByteArray→UUID, Long↔Int conversions

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Fix multi-database JDBC test compatibility

- ResultSetReader: use getBoolean() for BIT type (fixes Postgres/MySQL/MSSQL
  boolean handling), lowercase column names (fixes Oracle uppercase), fall
  through to getObject() for unknown types (fixes Oracle BINARY_DOUBLE)
- JdbcTransaction: map TypedNull to proper SQL types instead of Types.NULL
  (fixes MSSQL null binding)
- queryFor primitives: use convertSingleValue with type conversions instead
  of direct cast (fixes cross-database COUNT(*) type differences)
- Add default conversions: BigDecimal→Int/Long/Double/Boolean (Oracle NUMBER),
  Double→Int/Long

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* updated api dump

* Add JDBC driver-specific conversions and fix remaining test failures

- Add JdbcConversions with Oracle (Timestamp→LocalDate/LocalDateTime) and
  MSSQL (Int→Boolean) driver-specific conversions, mirroring R2DBC pattern
- Wire driver conversions through JdbcDataSource→JdbcTransaction→ResultSetReader→Row
- Use parameterMetaData.getParameterType() for null/TypedNull binding to get
  the correct SQL type from the prepared statement (fixes MSSQL and Oracle)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Remove Double→Int/Long default conversions

These lossy conversions don't make sense globally — silently truncating
doubles could cause subtle bugs. Oracle's BINARY_DOUBLE is already
handled correctly via getObject() returning Double.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Move BigDecimal conversions from global defaults to Oracle driver-specific

BigDecimal→Int/Long/Double/Boolean are only needed for Oracle's NUMBER
type and shouldn't apply globally where they could silently lose precision.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Fix review feedback: driver conversions in primitive queryFor, MySQL null binding

- Merge driverConversions in Row.convertSingleValue() for correct primitive queryFor
- Fix MySQL/MariaDB null binding by falling back to Types.NULL when parameterMetaData unavailable
- Remove redundant Types.OTHER branch in ResultSetReader
- Add SQL injection prevention test to basicInsertQueryTests factory
- Fix queryFor Int test to assert values instead of just size

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Update JDBC API dump

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Address PR review feedback

- Use getColumnLabel instead of getColumnName in ResultSetReader for alias support
- Use intValueExact/longValueExact in Oracle BigDecimal conversions

compiler now infers BOOL/DINT/REAL/STRING from literals, boolean ops,
comparisons, unary ops, arithmetic, and self.var references.

fix(e2e): blocked-periods date offset + bot webhook apikey (#364)

* feat(wheel-v3): migrate Bot E2E Anthropic button, fix GitHub 403

- Add Bot E2E (Anthropic) toggle to V3 with same visual as original wheel
- Uses /api/admin/bot-e2e-toggle to control BOT_E2E_ENABLED GitHub Actions variable
- Fix 403 error: add specific error message guiding user to reconfigure
  GH_ACTIONS_TOKEN with "Variables: Read and Write" scope
- Toggle disabled when error present, shows actionable error message

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(bot-e2e): use GH_PAT_ADMIN as primary token with fallback chain

Token priority: GH_PAT_ADMIN → GH_ACTIONS_TOKEN → GITHUB_TOKEN.
If a token returns 403, automatically tries the next one.
GH_PAT_ADMIN already has Variables scope, fixing the 403 in production.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(sync): rewrite sync-vercel route to use GitHub API instead of bash script

Vercel serverless functions cannot execute bash scripts. Replaced
exec('bash sync-to-vercel.sh') with direct GitHub REST API calls to
fetch commits, compare changes, check migrations and CI status.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(bot-e2e): send apikey header in webhook tests, skip validation when secret unconfigured

Bot E2E tests failed because sendBotMessage() didn't send the apikey
header, and WHATSAPP_WEBHOOK_SECRET was never configured in Vercel,
causing the webhook to reject ALL messages (including real ones).

Changes:
- sendBotMessage() now sends apikey header from WHATSAPP_WEBHOOK_SECRET
- validateEvolutionWebhook: skip validation when secret not configured
  (returns true + logs warning) instead of rejecting all requests
- CI: pass WHATSAPP_WEBHOOK_SECRET to all 4 bot E2E jobs
- Updated unit tests to match new behavior

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(e2e): use date offsets in blocked-periods test to avoid cross-week dates

farFutureWeekday(1), (2), (3) called independently could return dates
from different weeks. Use monday as base and calculate tuesday/wednesday
as +1/+2 day offsets to ensure they're in the same week. Also skip test
if both monday and wednesday have no slots.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat: use local event history to initialize lastChangeAt on first observation

On first API observation, check local event history (getEvents) for the player's
last recorded point change before falling back to estimator.lastBatchAt or null.

Priority: event history ts → lastBatchAt → null (UNKNOWN)

This allows users who previously tracked a player to get correct state immediately
on re-add, without waiting for a new batch detection.

Fix Canva blog post image loading for subpath pages

- Add middleware.ts to handle /_assets/* requests directly, replacing
  the unreliable next.config.js rewrite. Falls back to page-prefixed

  root /_assets/ returns 404, fixing broken images on subpath Canva pages.

  to canva-asset instead of stripping it.
- Relax canva-asset validation to accept paths containing /_assets/
  anywhere, not just at the start.

  detection and stabilise the dev server webpack cache.

Phase 27: GHL Conversations Intelligence + SMS Re-activation Campaigns

Add engagement intelligence engine that cross-references GHL pipeline data with
MindBody purchasing history and conversation analysis. Identifies engagement gaps,
lost revenue candidates, and speed-to-lead metrics across all 3 locations.

commit.message:
fix(backend): skip FedEx API responses with empty body, wait for second call

FedEx makes 2 calls to /track/v2/shipments — the first has an empty
body (CDP response.body undefined), the second has the actual data.
The settled guard was catching the first call's body parse error and
rejecting the promise, causing the second (valid) response to be
ignored.

Fix: catch json() parse errors silently and return (don't reject),
allowing the handler to process the next response. Only reject on
non-200 status or timeout.

Confirmed via VPS E2E test: both API calls return 200, first body
is empty, second has 11KB of tracking data.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: friendly error messages for API limit, rate limit, and model errors

The agent chat route was returning raw Anthropic API JSON error responses
(400 invalid_request_error) as 500 errors, showing ugly technical details
to users. Now detects and classifies error types:

- API usage limit → "service has reached monthly limit, restored April 1"
- Rate limit (429) → "temporarily rate-limited, try again in 30 seconds"
- Model not found → "model configuration needs updating"
- Missing API key → "system not fully configured"

Applied to both /api/agents/chat and /api/invest/analyze routes.
Also bumped agent chat timeout from 60s to 120s.
